working_definition: Type 2 conventional dendritic cell (cDC2) in adult human skin. Identified in Reynolds et al. (2021) as the DC2 population within the mononuclear phagocyte landscape. Defined by CD1c (BDCA-1), CLEC10A, CD11b, and IRF4 expression. Specialised for MHC class II antigen presentation to CD4+ T helper cells. Adult population; fetal DC2 is documented separately.

best_match.cl_definition: A conventional dendritic cell that expresses CD1c and CLEC10A and is specialised for MHC class II antigen presentation to CD4+ T helper cells. In human, also known as BDCA-1+/CD1c+ cDC.

justification: CL:0002400 'conventional dendritic cell type 2' directly corresponds to the DC2 population in Reynolds et al. (2021), confirmed by enrichment of murine dermal CD11c+ DC gene signatures and expression of CD1c, CLEC10A, and IRF4.
recommendation: Use CL:0002400 (conventional dendritic cell type 2) as an exact match for the DC2 cell type.
